title: Dockerfile and Compose Validation, Image Tests, Fault Injection, and Quality Gates · Stakeholder Container Mission
summary: A platform reliability group. The group needs a release candidate proven under slow dependencies, termination, read-only storage, resource pressure, clean-host startup, logs, health, and recoverable failure. Planned artifact: Deliver Fault-Tested Multi-Service Release Candidate with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Isolate client, daemon, build, image, process, storage, network, health, application, and host causes with competing hypotheses and a recovery record.
Test default user, entrypoint, arguments, files, permissions, labels, health, ports, signals, filesystem mutability, and exit behavior from the built image.
Inject slow start, crash, dependency refusal, DNS failure, read-only storage, full disk, termination, and resource limits into an isolated Compose project.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Project Docker Layered Incident Method
steps.instruction:
project case — A project image builds and answers one happy-path request but fails read-only execution, slow dependency startup, SIGTERM, missing secrets, and clean-host pull. Design layered tests plus deterministic teardown. Competency probe: Isolate client, daemon, build, image, process, storage, network, health, application, and host causes with competing hypotheses and a recovery record.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Project Docker Layered Incident Method.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for isolate client, daemon, build, image, process, storage, network, health, application, and host causes with competing hypotheses and a recovery record. For Commit to the mission model · Project Docker Layered Incident Method, keep Docker Layered Incident Method connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Docker Clean Host Transfer
steps.instruction:
Append the Docker evidence item headed "  # Evidence: docker-clean-host-transfer-n275r6". Tailor its container-test-plan.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run Docker commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Docker Clean Host Transfer.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Docker Clean Host Transfer into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Docker Clean Host Transfer, keep Docker Clean Host Transfer connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-clean-host-transfer-n275r6
  - competency: docker-clean-host-transfer
    artifact: container-test-plan.yaml
    decision: clean host transfer
    scenario-variant: 4
    environment: rootless remote builder on an arm64 staging host
    fault-injection: cancel during export after one platform finishes
    retained-proof: shutdown duration exit state and durable-work reconciliation
    case-key: n275r6
    change: |
      static: dockerfile-and-compose-validation
      image-contract: user-files-entrypoint-health
      integration: dependency-and-fault-injection
      transfer: clean-host-pull-and-run
      negative: readonly-slow-stop-missing-secret
    verify: docker buildx build --check . && docker compose config --quiet
    failure: Reject a successful build as runtime proof, a single happy-path curl, mutable local cache, or tests that depend on undeclared host state.
    repair: Layer structural, image, runtime, integration, fault, clean-host, and supply-chain checks with reproducible fixtures and teardown.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.
